title: Incidence-trend views with APC/AAPC + joinpoint caveats and screening-artifact flags
context: A key misreading of cancer statistics is interpreting incidence trends as indicating change in true cancer risk when they may reflect screening coverage changes (screening artifacts). This backlog task adds incidence-trend views with Annual Percent Change (APC) and Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) from joinpoint regression, with mandatory caveats about joinpoint methodology and mandatory screening-artifact flags where incidence trends are screening-sensitive. This overlaps with the sibling 'screening-coverage-trends' Elyos project. Statistics reviewer sign-off is required.
objective: Implement incidence-trend views (APC/AAPC from published joinpoint analyses) for the pilot cancer site, with mandatory joinpoint-methodology caveats and screening-artifact flags on all screening-sensitive trend series. GLOBOCAN estimates are not used for short-term trend claims. Every DataPoint carries full provenance. The trends views integrate with the existing statistics-correctness layer (lib/stats) and the caveat matrix. Patterns from the sibling 'screening-coverage-trends' project should be reused.
acceptanceCriteria: APC/AAPC trend views are implemented using published joinpoint analysis results (not novel modelling); every DataPoint carries full provenance
Mandatory joinpoint-methodology caveats are shown on all trend figures (e.g. joinpoint selection criteria, data vintage, the fact that short-term fluctuations can affect endpoints)

Screening-artifact flags are shown on all incidence-trend series for screening-sensitive cancer sites, explaining that rising incidence may reflect increased screening detection rather than increased true risk
GLOBOCAN estimates are not used for short-term trend claims (GLOBOCAN is cross-sectional modelled estimates, not longitudinal registry data)
CI and/or APC uncertainty are shown where the source publishes them
CI is green; reviewed and signed off by the Statistics reviewer
output: A merged PR adding incidence-trend views (APC/AAPC + joinpoint caveats + screening-artifact flags) integrated with the statistics-correctness layer, green CI, and statistics reviewer sign-off.
